name: Agentic AI: From Theory to Autonomous Systems
subtitle: Designing, Implementing, and Governing Next-Generation LLM Agents
description: This profile covers the evolution of autonomous AI agents powered by Large Language Models (LLMs), focusing on core architectural patterns like tool use, multi-agent collaboration, and self-correction. It spans from foundational concepts to advanced deployment challenges in embodied, software engineering, and complex simulation environments.
initial_queries: Multi-agent collaboration patterns in LLM applications
LLM agents for embodied simulation and exploration
Verbal Reinforcement Learning for language agents
Designing modular skill sets for LLMs
Provenance tracking in autonomous AI workflows
Energy efficiency of agentic issue resolution with SLMs

canonical_papers.title: ReAct: Synergizing Reasoning and Acting in Language Models
Voyager: An Open-Ended Embodied Agent with Large Language Models
Toolformer: Language Models Can Teach Themselves to Use Tools
Generative Agents: Interactive Simulacra of Human Behavior
AutoGen: Enabling Next-Gen LLM Applications via Multi-Agent Conversation

out_of_scope: General LLM Prompt Engineering (without agentic loop)
Specific Cybersecurity Incident Response (unless framed as an agent task)
Traditional Machine Learning Model Training (non-LLM based)

definition: What is the formal definition of an autonomous AI agent?
How do agents differ from simple prompt chains?
canonical: Comparing ReAct, Reflexion, and standard CoT prompting
Architectural blueprints for multi-agent systems using AutoGen
